title: A Thousand Like A Day

sections.label: Verse 1
lines: O Lord, You’ve been our dwelling place,
Through every age, Your boundless grace.
Before the mountains rose to stand,
You formed the world with Your mighty hand.

sections.label: Pre-Chorus
lines: From everlasting to eternity’s call,
You are God, the maker of all.

sections.label: Chorus
lines: A thousand years are but a day,
Like morning mist, they fade away.
Establish now the work we’ve done,
Let Your favor shine as the rising sun.

sections.label: Verse 2
lines: We’re fragile dust, yet You are near,
Through fleeting days, Your voice we hear.
Return, O Lord, and satisfy,
With mercy fresh as morning’s sky.

sections.label: Bridge
lines: So teach us, Lord, to count our days,
With hearts of wisdom, show Your ways.
Your beauty rests on all You make,
Forever strong, forever great.

sections.label: Chorus
lines: A thousand years are but a day,
Like morning mist, they fade away.
Establish now the work we’ve done,
Let Your favor shine as the rising sun.

sections.label: Final Chorus
lines: A thousand years are but a day,
Like morning mist, they fade away.
Establish now the work we’ve done,
Let Your favor shine as the rising sun.

sections.label: Outro
lines: From everlasting to everlasting,
Our refuge, our King, unchanging.

epigraph: or ever thou hadst formed the earth and the world
